name: Vinh Trinh
program: MS HCDE · University of Washington · Class of 2026
bio_formal: I'm a software engineer pursuing an MS in Human Centered Design & Engineering at the University of Washington. I've spent my career building production systems — shipping mobile apps, enterprise APIs, and cloud infrastructure — and I'm now expanding into UX research and human-centered design. I'm drawn to the space where engineering rigor meets user understanding: where the goal isn't just a system that works, but one that works for people.
bio_casual: Hey, I'm Vinh. By day I'm a software engineer; by night I'm an HCDE grad student at UW. I've shipped real products — production apps, cloud systems, a couple of patents — and I'm here to add the human side to that toolkit. I care about understanding why people use things the way they do, designing for the edges and not just the happy path, and building things that are thoughtful enough that nobody has to think about them. I also once spent two months filming a plant. It made a great 8-second video.
currently: Finishing my capstone project (see the countdown timer for my mental state)
Expanding from engineering into UX research and design
Probably spending too much time on this website
misc: Has strong opinions about font choices
Holds two US patents in the email/messaging space
Once spent 2 months filming a plant for an 8-second video — no regrets
Believes the best UX is the one nobody notices
